reports.title: The Iran Ceasefire That Neither Side Wants to Own
reports.subtitle: Trump extended the truce indefinitely but kept the blockade. Iran refused to send negotiators. Both sides are now waiting for the other to blink first.

reports.title: Trump Tells Companies to Waive Their Court-Ordered Refunds
reports.subtitle: The Supreme Court struck down his tariffs as unconstitutional. The refund portal is open. Trump is now pressuring companies to not use it, promising to 'remember' those who comply.

reports.title: Apple's Hardware Chief Takes Over. The Question Is Whether He Can Fix the Software Problem.
reports.subtitle: Tim Cook built a $3 trillion machine and leaves in September. John Ternus inherits it with no breakout AI product, a lagging Siri, and Jony Ive now working for OpenAI.

reports.title: The MATCH Act Would Let the US Cut Off Allies Who Don't Follow Its Chip Rules
reports.subtitle: Congress wants to force allies to comply with US semiconductor export controls or face secondary sanctions on any product using American technology. China says it has already adapted.

reports.title: Trump is Moving Against Birth Control. Quietly, Through a Federal Program.
reports.subtitle: The administration is transforming Title X, the $300M federal family planning program, away from contraception and toward 'natural family planning.' Planned Parenthood is being cut off. This is happening without legislation.

reports.title: Governments Are Banning Teens From Social Media. The Evidence Says It Won't Work.
reports.subtitle: Australia's under-16 ban, Canada's Liberal resolution, Missouri's new law, Indonesia's crackdown. A global wave of social media age restrictions is building. Every expert says enforcement will fail.

reports.title: OpenAI Gives the Government Access to Its Cyber Weapon. Anthropic's Already Leaked.
reports.subtitle: GPT-5.4-Cyber is being briefed to US agencies and Five Eyes partners under a 'dual-track' access model. Meanwhile, Anthropic's rival Mythos model was accessed by unauthorized users through a vendor the same week it launched.

reports.title: The Fifth Circuit Just Told Texas It Can Post the Ten Commandments in Every Public School. The Supreme Court Has to Take This.
reports.subtitle: A 9-8 ruling by the most conservative federal appeals circuit reversed a lower court injunction and greenlit Texas Senate Bill 10. ACLU is appealing. Louisiana and Arkansas have similar laws waiting.

reports.title: 86% of US CEOs Are Planning as If Trump's Tariffs Will Outlast Trump
reports.subtitle: A PwC survey of 633 executives found the planning assumption has shifted: tariffs are no longer a negotiating tactic to wait out. They are structural. The Supreme Court struck down IEEPA tariffs. Congress didn't replace them. CEOs adapted anyway.

reports.title: A Federal Judge Ordered Trump to Unblock Wind and Solar. The Permits Were Being Delayed While Fossil Fuels Carried On.
reports.subtitle: Chief Judge Denise Casper in Boston issued a preliminary injunction against five Interior Department and Army Corps policies that put wind and solar permitting in a separate, slower track from other energy sources.

reports.title: AT&T and Verizon Want a Jury Trial for Their FCC Fines. The Supreme Court Seems Skeptical But Interested.
reports.subtitle: FCC v. AT&T asks whether the Seventh Amendment requires a jury trial before a federal agency can impose a nine-figure fine. The answer would reshape how every regulatory agency in the US enforces penalties.
